item.title: Hey is this true? It would solve a lot of people's problems.
item.dc:creator: LunarWhiteLeopard

item.title: Bottleneck Theory
item.description: - Research suggests a severe human population bottleneck occurred around 900,000 years ago, reducing numbers to about 1,280 breeding individuals for roughly 117,000 years, likely due to climate change. - The evidence leans toward these survivors being ancestors of all modern humans, marking a critical evolutionary point. - There is some debate about the exact timing and causes, with studies showing varied estimates and methodologies. Study Overview The study, titled "Genomic inference of a severe human bottleneck during the Early to Middle Pleistocene transition," was published in *Science* on August 31, 2023, by Wangjie Hu et al. It used genomic analysis to suggest this bottleneck, potentially triggered by climate shifts, and its lasting impact on human evolution. Access and Further Reading You can access the full study at [Science Journal](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.abq7487). Additional context is available from news articles like [Popular Science](https://www.popsci.com/science/human-population-pleistocene/) and [Sci.News](https://www.sci.news/othersciences/anthropology/pleistocene-human-bottleneck-12232.html). Comprehensive Survey Note This detailed survey note explores the study referenced in the attachment, which describes a significant event in human evolutionary history: a severe population bottleneck around 900,000 years ago. The analysis is based on the attachment's content, which was a "Fact Point" post summarizing a DNA study, and subsequent research to identify the original publication and its findings. Background and Context The attachment, a "Fact Point" post dated one day prior to the analysis (likely from June 22, 2025, given the current date of June 23, 2025), featured a social media-style layout with a lightbulb icon and branding. It included a detailed description of a DNA study suggesting that around 800,000 years ago, early humans faced a population bottleneck, dropping to as few as 1,280 individuals. This event lasted approximately 117,000 years and was potentially caused by drastic climate change, with survivors eventually repopulating and becoming ancestors of all modern humans. The post also included a visual of an ancient human skull, reinforcing the evolutionary context. The post's text highlighted key points: a timeframe of approximately 800,000 years ago, a population reduction to 1,280 individuals, a duration of 117,000 years, and a likely cause of climate change. The visual elements, such as the skull and colorful gradient design, emphasized the archaeological and educational nature of the content. Identifying the Study To find the specific study, the focus was on matching the described findings with published research. The attachment did not name the researchers, institutions, or publication, so a web search was conducted using keywords like "DNA study human population bottleneck 800,000 years ago 1,280 individuals climate change." This led to multiple news articles from August 31, 2023, referencing a study published in *Science* with similar details. Further exploration confirmed the study as "Genomic inference of a severe human bottleneck during the Early to Middle Pleistocene transition" by Wangjie Hu et al., published in *Science* on August 31, 2023, with the DOI 10.1126/science.abq7487. The study used a new model called FitCoal to analyze genomic sequences from 3,154 individuals, suggesting a bottleneck between approximately 930,000 and 813,000 years ago, lasting about 117,000 years, with the population dropping to 1,280 breeding individuals Detailed Findings The study's findings align closely with the attachment's summary, though there are slight variations in the estimated timeframe (930,000–813,000 years ago versus the attachment's 800,000 years ago). It suggests that about 98.7% of the ancestral population was lost at the beginning of this bottleneck, potentially triggered by major climate change. This event is believed to have coincided with subsequent speciation events, possibly leading to the emergence of Homo heidelbergensis, a shared ancestor of modern humans, Neanderthals, and Denisovans. The methodology involved analyzing present-day human genomic sequences, which allowed researchers to infer past population sizes and genetic diversity. The study posits that the survivors of this bottleneck were critical to the repopulation and eventual dominance of modern humans, marking a pivotal moment in evolutionary history. Supporting Evidence and Controversy Multiple sources, including [Popular Science](https://www.popsci.com/science/human-population-pleistocene/), [Sci.News](https://www.sci.news/othersciences/anthropology/pleistocene-human-bottleneck-12232.html), [The Guardian](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2023/aug/31/population-collapse-almost-wiped-out-human-ancestors-say-scientists), [Scientific American](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/human-ancestors-nearly-went-extinct-900-000-years-ago/), and [Natural History Museum](https://www.nhm.ac.uk/discover/news/2023/august/human-ancestors-may-have-almost-died-out-ancient-population-crash.html), corroborate these findings, with estimates ranging from 800,000 to 930,000 years ago for the bottleneck's onset. However, there is some debate about the exact timing and causes, with some experts questioning the precision of genomic models and the fossil record's gaps during this period. For instance, [Scientific American](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/human-ancestors-nearly-went-extinct-900-000-years-ago/) notes that the patchy fossil record between 950,000 and 650,000 years ago may explain the chronological gap, while [The Guardian](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2023/aug/31/population-collapse-almost-wiped-out-human-ancestors-say-scientists) mentions that the bottleneck could have triggered new species emergence, adding complexity to the interpretation. Comparative Analysis To organize the variations in reported details, the following table summarizes key aspects from the attachment and the identified study: | Aspect | Attachment Summary | Study Findings (Hu et al., 2023) | |-------------------------|---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------| | Timeframe | Around 800,000 years ago | 930,000–813,000 years ago | | Population Size | As few as 1,280 individuals | About 1,280 breeding individuals | | Duration | Approximately 117,000 years | Approximately 117,000 years | | Cause | Drastic climate change | Likely major climate change | | Outcome | Survivors ancestors of all modern humans | Survivors critical to modern human ancestry | This table highlights the alignment in population size and duration, with slight differences in the estimated start date, possibly due to rounding or interpretation in the "Fact Point" post. Implications and Further Research The study's implications are significant for understanding human evolution, suggesting that a near-extinction event shaped genetic diversity and species development. It also raises questions about resilience to climate change, with parallels to current environmental challenges. Further research could explore the fossil record's gaps or refine genomic models to narrow the timeframe and causes. For access to the original study, the URL [Science Journal](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.abq7487) provides the full paper, while news articles offer accessible summaries for lay audiences. BoKey Citations - [Human population bottleneck 800,000 years ago](https://www.popsci.com/science/human-population-pleistocene/) - [Early human ancestors bottleneck study](https://www.sci.news/othersciences/anthropology/pleistocene-human-bottleneck-12232.html) - [Population collapse of human ancestors](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2023/aug/31/population-collapse-almost-wiped-out-human-ancestors-say-scientists) - [Human ancestors near extinction 900,000 years ago](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/human-ancestors-nearly-went-extinct-900-000-years-ago/) - [Human ancestors population crash news](https://www.nhm.ac.uk/discover/news/2023/august/human-ancestors-may-have-almost-died-out-ancient-population-crash.html)

item.title: Yep, it's not because we hate all men and need to be 'tamed' or whatever BS, it's because we're trying to stay alive! We can love men, have great ones in our lives, and also know there are dangerous people out there!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: Hyatt Just Reportedly Fired Nearly Hundreds Of US Call Center Agent Overnight—Now Your Calls Are Answered For $400/Month In El Salvador
item.description: Hyatt has reportedly laid off hundreds of remaining U.S. phone customer service agents. Last week it appears that roughly 300 U.S. agents were terminated, and there’s a report that there seem to be just 35 left. The hotel chain is also said to have dismissed 18 managers and the bulk of the U.S. chat team, leaving about 36 U.S. chat agents left. Employees apparently received 24 hours notice before termination. There are no more physical U.S. call centers, with remaining local agents remote. Several self-identified call center employees reported on their layoff experience. I went from joining a zoom call with the director of my team, to being forced to turn on my camera, to being told I was laid off effective literally immediately, to then having an HR rep say “you’re going to need to take notes now”, to me just saying. I’m sure all of this info is going to be in an email you’ll be sending to my personal address so please can I get off this call now?” They literally shoved me out the door, sat there staring at me as I was crying and humiliated and confused. Then the realization hit me — how am I going to pay for my mortgage? Health Insurance? ETc. All while the HR rep and the temporary director of my team sat there in silence. …a 5 minute zoom to lay us off with literally NO advance warning or foreshadowing. They literally said your Hyatt email is going to deactivate in about an hour. https://viewfromthewing.com/hyatt-just-reportedly-fired-nearly-every-us-call-center-agent-overnight-now-your-calls-are-answered-for-400-month-in-el-salvador/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: The 526th vol. of "I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps - Pride Month 2025 ep 1
item.description:
Just 6 Dump Days left of SUPER GAY MEEMAYS!
STAY TUNED! I'll be releasing more MeeMays back into the wild *every other* Monday-Friday around 11:30 am, GMT-5, in my ""I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so that I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps" Or use Imgur's "Follow" Feature, which will alert you either when I post or when I go Viral. If you wish to chat, I'm frequently in ElbowDeepInAHorse (The Stable) and PotatoCheesMonk's (Deviousness) Discord Servers between the hours of 7pm and 1am GMT-5 during the week, and at unpredictable but frequent times Saturdays and Sundays. https://discord.gg/JoinTheStable https://discord.gg/Deviousness These are both VERY safe places for LGBTQIA+ people, if you need a community, please stop in! AbbiiStabbii's server is also amazing, *especially* safe for Trans folk. @PlanckEraWasMyBestEra also has a server for Parents who are Trans https://discord.me/transparents
Definitely not named poorly It's actually quite a heartwarming story of how Subaru became a Lesbian icon. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subaru_of_America#Popularity_in_the_LGBT_community (See the references at the bottom)
@TheSmogMonster Ace Meme!
I'll be in the weed smoking room
@ElbowDeepInAHorse
@AlwaysUpvotesCatgirls
@TheCunningLinguists
This is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq Updated Whenever I Can Get Around To It #LGBTQIA #PrideMonth #Pride_Month #Pride #LGBTPride #Phone_Dump #Meme_Dump #Memes #MeeMays #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple #GregFromHR #SteveFromAccounting #DougFromSalesThis is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq Updated Whenever I Can Get Around To It #LGBTQIA #PrideMonth #Pride_Month #Pride #LGBTPride #Phone_Dump #Meme_Dump #Memes #MeeMays #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple #GregFromHR #SteveFromAccounting #DougFromSales

item.title: Canada changed its fate today
item.description: Canada is pivoting decisively away from the US, building a new partnership with Europe, and carving out its own place in the world. We would have been fine carrying on as America’s bestie, but you guys dumped us so we’re joining the European polycule. See ya! https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/jun/23/canada-eu-defense-trump-us-allies
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: In Finland, unlike other European cultures, trivial conversation or “small talk” are almost non-existent. In this country, not only is it not encouraged, but there is not even a concept of it in the Finnish language.
item.description: ????? In Finland, unlike other European cultures, trivial conversation or “small talk” are almost non-existent. In this country, not only is it not encouraged, but there is not even a concept of it in the Finnish language. Many children, when learning English, discover this type of interaction as something expected when talking to foreigners. At social gatherings, the Finns do not feel the need to fill the silences; often, these can last for minutes without causing discomfort. This preference for silence extends to other parts of the Nordic countries, such as Trondheim or Tromsø. Finnish culture values meaningful conversations and, instead of filling each pause with empty words, they prefer calm. So, if you ever find yourself in a social situation in Finland, remember that silence is not something negative, but a space for reflection, and better to avoid starting conversations about the climate.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: It shall be so!
item.description:
So I have “verbal” confirmation that @TinyOctopus is going to adopt Frank!!
I am absolutely titillated that this is going to happen! Not only will Frank be going to an amazing forever home, but we will get to watch this little bugger grow up in the home of an Imgurian legend!! AND I will get to meet Tiny face-to-face!! It couldn’t be a better situation!! @FrankTheKitten is my new favorite account!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: You need to cool... and to get some memes
item.description:
You have no power here
5
I have that feeling when playing any game made with Unity
Okay it's the third one but it's sorted by download and it's funny
Is happiness even reachable in the present or does it lie in the past ?
10
Please don't do that. And for the love of all that is sacred and precious comment your code like you need to explain it to a three year old
It's at least 4 stars rare
15
20. Use this one well as a reaction meme
It's a peaceful protest... For now
25. Ominous evil horse
Taxes !
30
That's it for today ! Have a good day ! And Week ! After that we'll see. Checkout my profile for other memes, or wait a week or so for more chaos. I saw a lot of dumper using signatures' pictures at the end of their memes dump. Shall I do that too ? A ping list ? Not sure how imgur works tbh. Are some of you even humans ? Are we gonna make it as a species ? Is there a character limit to comments on a picture ? If yes can I comment the entire bee movie script under a random picture ?

item.title: "Noir Nights" My latest acrylic painting. 20" x 16"
item.description:
I know these scenes are a little cliche, but I really love painting them. Here's my latest one!! Going through a heatwave here in Canada right now so I felt like doing a rainy scene. #art #creativity #crafts #rain #painting
And for those who like to look at my colorful paint desk :)

item.title: Memes/tomfoolery
item.description:
I've always wanted a bumper sticker that said "my other car's made of Legos"
Then plugs in his apple
All this dude wanted to convey is that he knows where his boss lives and that he's completely unhinged.

sound on.
sound on here too.
take a deep breath before moving on.
this is what should have happened.
the guy at the bar doesn't even flinch.
the audio obviously doesn't match.
sound on for this one.

item.title: ICM QUALITY MIX - Vol.53 EIGHT IS ENOUGH

COVER ARTIST: Kopfscheu
ICM SUGGESTED TRACK: [ ▶ & SCROLL] Too Much - Tora YouTube: https://youtu.be/3U5Z4oeOAp8?si=Sa-yVF7hrxviGg9b Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/track/1Bb8NUOSX6jEdjLtR5TEuQ?si=4eaebb88b00f4180

Suggested Track Playlists: (HIT SHUFFLE) YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoIzKPbKfwniKOaadw-tB8kaEQiUQ1Yp4 Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/6cqV52puia81vNnPHxcz1W?si=yoUknxpQQVql9E-NZRQ4 ICM SQUAD: @vazquezdaniel, @ohvalleyofplenty, @nichoDriver, @Dirgekins, @onewallpaperaweek, @thedownlowe, @TheForbiddenSemicolon, @bagoffishstix, @JayKillBam, @majkongskull, @DingoJ0nes, @HandsomePenguin, @MrJwinkyface, @Gorgoror, @ciadmillefailte, @kaeldra ICM TIP JAR: https://www.patreon.com/InterwebChexMix Please note that we do not claim ownership of any random art, comics, or photos featured in this collection; we only take credit for the daily effort that goes into compiling the collections themselves. Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos

item.title: Demonstration of a traditional Chinese water-lifting device known as the "Xijun Shuihu," historically used for drawing water from wells.
item.dc:creator: bohund
